model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, the baseline under-utilizes data exposure and likely under-trains early. Doubling batch size increases examples seen per verifier budget, and a modest LR increase keeps optimization progress per step strong without adding code complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The cosine schedule over 256 steps hurt validation loss, so reverting to constant LR restores the stronger short-horizon optimization behavior seen in the best run.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current setup likely remains mildly capacity-limited at 256 steps; a small width increase should improve feature quality with minimal complexity and usually stable optimization under Adam at the current learning rate.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After improving convolutional width, slightly enlarging the classifier head is a low-complexity capacity match that can reduce underfitting within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The larger head regressed validation loss, so reverting to the smaller classifier keeps the beneficial convolutional-width gain while removing unhelpful extra capacity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With short-horizon training and increased model width, AdamW often gives better early generalization than Adam at the same nominal hyperparameters by decoupling weight decay from adaptive updates.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After switching to AdamW and improving capacity, a modest LR increase can improve progress per step in the fixed 256-step regime while staying in a conservative stability range.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"In this short fixed-step regime, slightly weaker regularization can improve fit quality before overfitting becomes dominant, especially with AdamW already providing decoupled decay.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The previous step showed lower decay helps this short-budget setup; pushing decay down further is a targeted continuation to test whether the model is still under-regularized at 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Further reducing weight decay regressed loss, so reverting to 5e-5 restores the best-known regularization level for this configuration.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Recent improvements suggest the run is still optimization-limited at 256 steps; a moderate additional LR increase is the simplest high-upside next probe.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss kept improving as LR increased from 1.2e-3 to 1.4e-3, so the next efficient probe is a further modest step up to test whether the short-horizon optimum is still higher.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Validation loss continues to improve with each LR increase, so the highest-value next experiment is another small upward step to locate the short-budget optimum.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Increasing LR to 1.8e-3 caused a clear regression, so reverting to 1.6e-3 restores the best-performing learning-rate setting found so far.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 160","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With fixed training steps, increasing batch size raises total examples seen; a moderate bump should improve short-horizon data efficiency while keeping optimization behavior close to the current best setup.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 160","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Increasing batch size to 160 caused a major regression, so reverting to 128 restores the strong optimization/generalization balance.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After a clear drop at 1.8e-3 and best-so-far at 1.6e-3, a fine-grained local sweep just below 1.6e-3 is a high-value final tuning move.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With a short 256-step budget, lowering AdamW beta2 can improve adaptation speed and early optimization, potentially reducing validation loss at the current near-optimal LR.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Lowering beta2 to 0.99 significantly improved short-horizon learning, so retesting the previously strong 1.6e-3 LR under this faster-adapting optimizer state is the highest-upside final probe.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}
